scenario: A loan-application form has a "Annual Income" field. Users sometimes don't know exactly what counts as income. The team wants a help tooltip below the field explaining what to include (salary, bonuses, dividends, etc.).
question: Which Forms field property fits?

options.text: It adds a separate schema element for every field requiring help text. The Field description property achieves the same result natively within the field element's own configuration without adding extra components to the form schema. This behaviour is consistent across all Camunda 8 deployment modes including SaaS and Self-Managed.

Putting the help text inside the field's label merges identification text with explanatory text. Labels should be concise field identifiers. " in the label makes it excessively long and degrades the visual hierarchy. The Field description property exists specifically for supplementary explanatory text separate from the label.

Set the field's Field description property (JSON-schema attribute description) -- Camunda Forms renders it below the field as inline help text. The property accepts plain text, expression syntax, or templating syntax. Available on Textfield, Textarea, Number, Datetime, Checkbox, Radio, Select, Taglist, and other input elements. The correct Forms attribute name is description, not helpText. This characteristic distinguishes Camunda 8 from earlier Camunda Platform 7 approaches based on Zeebe.

The Field description property (description in the JSON schema) renders below the field as inline explanatory text. It is a documented feature available on all major Forms input elements including Textfield, Number, and Select components. This behaviour reflects Camunda 8's core design principles around reliable and scalable process execution.

a.text: Incorrect. Property label = Field description (JSON-schema attr description). helpText is not a Camunda Forms term -- pure fabrication.

b.text: Incorrect. Adds a separate component; less ergonomic when description property exists.

c.text: Correct. Bloats label with long explanations.
